title: THE POWER OF PRAYER | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: Thank you.
Hare Krishna.
Warm welcome to all of you for today's Gita Live session.
The topic for the day is the power of prayers.
It's a very interesting topic.
Today we will learn why it is important for us to offer prayers.
Some do's and don'ts about the prayers.
All of us do pray in one form or another.
But sometimes, who knows, unknowingly, unintentionally, we may be praying the wrong way.
So we will talk about the do's and don'ts.
And towards the end also I would like to share some authorized prayers.
Although prayers mean something which we express from our heart,
words really don't matter, it's the feelings, it's the bhava, which actually is the main thing.
But nevertheless, I will share with you some authorized prayers.
So before we get into this discussion, let's all fold our hands and offer prayers together.
Seek the blessings of Srila Prabhupada, Lord Sri Krishna, Lord Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and get into this discussion.
So please fold your hands and offer prayers.
And join me in offering the prayers together.
Nama Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale
Srimate Bhaktivedanta Swaminitinamine
Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine
Nirvishesha Shunyavadi
Pashchatyade Shatarine
Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya
Prabhu Nithyananda
Sri Advaita Gadadhara
Sri Vasadi Gaurabhakta Vrinda
Namo Mahavadanyaya
Krishna Prema Pradayate
Krishnaya Krishna Chaitanya
Namne Gauratuse Namaha
Namo Brahmane Devaya
Go Brahmane Hitaya Cha
Jagadh Hitaya Krishnaya
Govindaya Namo Namaham
He Krishna Karuna Sindho
Dinabandhu Jagatpate
Gopesha Gopika Kanta
Radha Kanta Namostute
Tapta Kanchan Gaurangi
Radhe Vrindavaneshwari
Vrishabhanusute Devi
Pranamami Hari Priye
Vanchakalpaturubhyasya
Kripasindhubhyaevachaa
Patitanam Paavnebhyo
Vaishnavebhyo
Namo Namaha
Hare Krishna Hare Krishna
Krishna Krishna Hare Hare
Hare Rama Hare Rama
Rama Rama Hare Hare
Krishna Krishna Hare Hare
Krishna Krishna Hare Hare
So the first aspect which we will talk about today is the importance of prayers.
Why we all need to pray on a regular basis?
What is the importance? What is the need?
For any relationship to develop, we need to communicate.
Communication is very important.
Communication is the key to nurturing a good relationship.
So likewise Lord Krishna is a person.
He is not some abstract force, some power.
He is a person with feelings, with emotions.
And Krishna in the Gita says,
mamaivamso jiva loke jivabhuta sanatana
We are his amshas.
We are his part and parcels.
We have a relationship with him.
That's a different thing that today we have forgotten that relationship.
We are not very conscious of that relationship.
Just like a child may forget who his father is, but still the father does exist.
He has a father.
He may not remember him.
He may not be grateful to him.
He may not be obligated to him.
He may forget him, but still the father does exist.
So likewise Krishna says,
mamaivamso jiva loke jivabhuta sanatana
We are his amshas.
We are his part and parcel.
We have a relationship with him.
But unfortunately today we have forgotten that relationship.
That relationship has covered our attention to him.
Our consciousness which we should be thinking of him is now absorbed in thinking of so many other things.
So if we have to nurture that relationship,
then we need to on a regular basis communicate, speak.
Just like any relationship between husband and wife,
let's say father and a child,
they talk to each other.
They communicate to each other.
So likewise,
prayer is an important means by which we communicate with our supreme father,
Lord Shri Krishna.
Prayer is also important because many a times in our lives,
we will come across some dilemmas,
some confusion,
some doubts.
We will be on crossroads,
knowing which path to choose.
An example being Arjuna in the battlefield of Kurukshetra.
He was in a dilemma.
He was in confusion whether he should fight this war or not.
So in that confused bewildered state,
he approached the Lord,
revealed his mind,
revealed his confusion,
revealed his dilemma and sought clarity.
So prayers are also important.
Because it can give us clarity.
It can help us resolve dilemmas.
We all will go through doubts,
confusions,
challenges in life,
and we will need direction.
So prayer is an important tool
or an important medium by which we can seek that direction,
clarity from the Lord.
Having faith that the Lord is,
the Lord is all knowledgeable.
The Lord is all knowing.
He knows everything.
He knows the past.
He knows the present.
He knows the future.
He is there in everyone's heart.
He knows what is good for us.
He is the supreme creator.
He is the source of all knowledge.
So with that faith,
we can seek direction from the Lord.
Now I am a soul surrendered unto you.
Arjuna tells Krishna,
please instruct me.
So we can,
in our prayers,
seek direction from the Lord
and seek his advice,
his instructions,
his guidance.
So that's the second aspect.
One is communication
to nurture the relationship.
And any relationship required,
requires communication,
some interaction.
Second is,
when we need some directions,
guidance.
Third is,
sheer glorification.
Lord has given us so many things.
Some we are conscious of,
some we have taken for granted.
For example,
even small insignificant things
like the air we breathe,
the water we drink,
the sunlight,
the food we eat,
seldom we stop and we thank the Lord.
We have taken it for granted
as if it's our birthright.
For example,
we have taken it for granted
that we can go anywhere,
we can meet anyone,
we can do anything.
But during lockdown,
we got some iota of that understanding
that how we are limited.
We have limitations.
Suddenly the conditions can change
and our so-called freedom,
so-called independence can be curtailed.
See what's happening in Afghanistan.
What's happening to millions of Afghans
who are now refugees
in different countries.
So,
prayer is also an important way
whereby we can express our gratitude,
our thanks
for all what the Lord has done for us
and continuing to do for us.
All the things which He has blessed us with.
So prayer is an important medium
to express our gratitude, our thanks.
Prayer is also important
because at times when we feel weak,
when we are in a difficult situation,
we all will need strength,
we all will need help,
we all will need some support.
And at times,
in spite of having everything,
we will feel that
we are helpless.
Just yesterday I was speaking to a gentleman
who came to meet me.
He was talking about that
this was during the second wave,
May month,
when it was peak of the infections
and all the hospital beds were all full,
there was shortage of oxygen.
So a person who is very very rich,
filthy rich,
his son about 22 years old
started having breathing problems.
He was sick,
he was diagnosed with COVID
and he had breathing issues.
So he was from a relatively smaller city
close to Jaipur.
And this person rushed to Jaipur
and on his way
called up this person whom I was speaking to yesterday
and had said that
I am coming to Jaipur,
please do something,
arrange a bed for me,
arrange a bed for my son,
he is sick,
he needs oxygen support.
So the person said
wait, wait, wait,
let me just check up the availability,
just wait.
He says no,
I have started from my home,
I am moving towards Jaipur,
please arrange,
do something.
He is in bad state.
And he also said
money is not an issue,
I am carrying with me 20 lakh cash.
Anything is required,
you want to pay to the hospital,
you want to pay to the doctor,
you want to pay to the nursing home,
pay for any medicine,
there is no shortage of money,
I am carrying with me 20 lakh rupees cash.
I am on my way to Jaipur,
please do something.
Now this was the force,
imagine the circumstances.
So this person frantically starts calling
the doctors whom he knows,
starts calling the IS officers whom he knows,
but nobody is lifting his hand.
But nobody is lifting the calls.
The doctors who have worked under him,
they are not lifting the calls.
Some near and dear ones
who are in the medical profession,
they are not lifting the calls.
The regular doctors,
the family doctors
whom he regularly visits,
good acquaintance,
they are not lifting the calls.
So he is feeling helpless.
And then this person lands up
in Jaipur.
There is no bed available.
Finally with great difficulty,
they could manage to get into a hospital
and that son for some time
had to be put on oxygen support
in a corridor of the hospital.
So this person was telling,
for first time in my life,
I saw that in spite of having
the best of contacts,
in spite of having the money,
in spite of having good relationships,
I saw that it was all,
I was so helpless.
Luckily that person could get
that oxygen support and
he could recover.
So the next question I was asking,
did he recover?
22 year old boy.
So since then this person also has
become quite religious
and he was telling that how
he has little bit calmed down.
Otherwise he was very passionate.
Money, money, money, more money.
So he says I have calmed down a bit.
I have seen that when the time comes,
the time comes,
these are all not going to help.
So we all will go through phases.
The other day our doctor called me
and her sister is going through
major surgery.
She is diagnosed with cancer
and major surgery.
And she called up,
although she is a very eminent doctor,
helps us quite often.
We also consult her.
But she was
needing some help,
some support,
some strength.
Her sister was about to
get into the ICU
and the preparations were on
for the surgery,
operation theatre.
And she called me.
So we also need prayers for strength,
for support.
At times we will feel weak.
At times we will feel incapable.
In spite of that,
in spite of having everything,
we will feel that they are not enough.
Srimad Bhagavatam says that
this Sainya, the soldiers,
Deha Aapta Kalatra Deshu
Atma Saineshu Asat Swapi
Deha means this good, strong, stout body.
Aapta, relatives, friend circles.
Kalatra Deshu, good, loving wife.
Children, relatives,
near and dear ones.
Kalatra Adishu.
Adishu means et cetera, like that.
When we are surrounded by an army
of well-wishers,
with lot of money,
with lot of support,
backed with insurance policies,
we get a feeling that we are now safe,
we are now secure.
Bhagavatam says,
Asat Sainya.
These Sainya, these soldiers,
which we have gathered around us,
to protect us,
they are Asat Sainya,
they are fallible soldiers.
When the actual war will start,
when the actual time will come,
you will see that these Sainya,
the soldiers,
will not be capable,
will have limitation in how much
they can support us,
how much they can protect us.
Tesham Pramata Nidhanam Pashyan
Apeena Pashyati
A person who is maddened,
who is in illusion,
who is bewildered with Deha Aapta,
Deha means I and mine,
who is in this illusion.
Tesham Pramata Nidhanam
He doesn't think that eventually one day
death will come
and all of them will be helpless.
I will have to surrender to death
in spite of having so many protective layers around me.
I will have to surrender to death in spite of having so many protective layers around me.
I will have to surrender to death in spite of having so many protective layers around me.
This is statement of the Bhagavatam.
So, we all will need strength.
We all will feel helpless at times.
And then,
so this is another thing we need prayers for.
And then we need prayers also,
just to,
just to,
as I said, communication.
We all have,
when we get up in the morning,
we have some rituals.
Yes, it's very very important
that we spend time with the Lord.
We spend time
with our loving Father.
Even if we don't have any agenda,
just like
the family gets together and talks to each other,
there is no well-defined agenda,
there is no particular thing
to achieve.
They just spend time together
because they love each other.
Prayer is also
an important aspect where we just
spend time with the Lord
with no particular agenda.
There is no checklist,
there is no tick marks to be done.
We just want to
spend time with our loving Father.
Be in His presence,
in His association.
This also nurtures the relationship.
As I said,
investing time,
investing energy,
investing feelings,
interacting,
these all are important aspects
whereby any relationship gets nurtured.
So these are the few reasons.
There are many more reasons.
I just thought before
I could share few of these reasons
why it is important to incorporate prayers
in our daily schedule.
Another reason,
another important aspect
which we are going to cover today is
what are the important aspects of prayers?
What are the important components of the prayers?
As I said, prayer is very important.
But what constitutes a good prayer?
What is the kind of prayer which we offer
will be pleasing to the Lord?
Lord will be moved by such a prayer.
What are the kind of prayers we can offer?
So,
there are many components.
There can be many aspects
which can be incorporated in the prayers.
But I will just highlight few important ones.
First and foremost,
prayers should
be backed with faith.
Faith that the Lord is listening to my prayers.
If you are offering prayers with doubts,
I am offering prayers,
don't know whether Lord is hearing or not hearing,
not sure whether He will respond,
He will reciprocate,
He will answer my prayers,
He will answer my prayers.
So, one of the very important components of prayer is faith.
Yes, Lord does exist.
Yes, Lord is my loving Father.
Yes, He listens to me.
Now, somebody may have that faithlessness
that Lord is Supreme Lord,
millions and billions of living entities,
I am small insignificant person,
why will He listen to me?
There are so many great devotees,
Lord is busy with them,
He is interacting with them,
who will hear my voice?
Just like a person can think,
how can I meet the Prime Minister of the country?
How will He listen to me?
Why will He listen to me?
Such limitations don't exist in the Lord.
The Lord has the ability
to listen to each and every Jeeva,
each and every person.
Sometimes, you know, when we are,
when there are a lot of people reporting to us or something,
at times somebody wants our time,
but we are not able to give up,
we give our time,
because we ourselves are so busy.
We find that, yes, we have limitation,
but that's not the limitation with the Lord.
So, with that faith,
faith is a very important component,
where we have the faith that,
yes, the Lord exists,
He listens to my prayers,
He is powerful enough to reciprocate,
and if He wills,
if He wills,
He will definitely help me with whatever I am requesting,
whatever I am asking in prayer.
So, faith is a very important component.
So, when we start prayers,
there are some prayers which reiterate,
reinforce that faith,
yes, Lord,
You are the Supreme Controller,
You are the cause of all causes,
You are omnipotent,
You are all-powerful.
These phrases, these prayers,
reinforces our faith,
our conviction that,
yes, Lord, You are there,
and You are listening to such prayers.
Another important aspect,
it's not right that we simply start
talking to the Lord.
It is important that when we offer prayers,
we go through our Guru,
the spiritual Master,
and then Gauranga,
Chaitanya Mahaprabhu,
and then the previous Acharyas,
and then to the Lord.
It is not okay to start with prayer
directly speaking to the Lord,
O Lord, please give me this, give me that,
O Lord, You are great.
Whenever we offer prayers,
whenever we have a session,
we are praying to the Lord,
it is always important,
just like even the session we start,
we start with Nama, Om, Vishnu, Pada,
we offer prayer to Shila Prabhu,
we offer prayer to Chaitanya Mahaprabhu,
we offer prayers to previous Acharyas,
we offer prayers to Krishna.
So we have to go in that order.
Just like if you get an appointment with,
a crude example,
with Narendra Modi,
for getting that appointment,
you have to go through a process.
You have to speak to someone who is close to him.
He in turn will speak to somebody
in the Prime Minister's office,
his secretary or whatever,
and then he in turn will speak to the Prime Minister,
and he will communicate,
and that thing will come down
to that person.
Although yes, we can speak to the Lord,
but it's a thing,
it's a good etiquette that we go through
our spiritual master, through the Guru,
who is in turn dear to Krishna.
When we go through Guru,
when he recommends, when he,
for example, somebody is very powerful
and is very close to Narendra Modi,
when we approach him,
that I want to meet him,
then for sure if he recommends our case
to Narendra Modi or the Prime Minister,
definitely he will give an appointment.
So, it's always good that we go through proper channel,
like even in the government circles,
proper channel of moving the file.
So, we go through a proper channel.
Another important component of the prayer is
that we simply don't start off
with,
Oh Lord, I am feeling weak,
I need your help, I need your guidance.
That's not the right way.
If you see all the authorized prayers in the Bhagavatam,
we have prayers of Kunti Maharani,
prayers of Dhruva Maharaj,
prayers of Prahlad Maharaj,
prayers of Ambarish Maharaj,
so many great devotees.
You will always see the prayers
starting with glorification of the Lord.
They are just glorifying the Lord.
It is not some maska bhaji,
but it is just out of sheer love,
appreciation of the wonderful qualities of the Lord.
Glorification of the Lord.
So, another important component of the prayer is glorification.
Glorifying the qualities of the Lord,
exalted qualities of the Lord.
The way the Lord reciprocates with His devotees.
And this trait, that when someone says,
and this trait, that when someone appreciates us,
someone praises us, we feel good.
We are amsa of the Lord,
this trait is also there in its pure form in the Lord.
Any person, when we praise him,
or when we glorify him, he feels good.
In fact, lot of people are doing many things for glorification,
so that they get appreciated, they get glorified,
people praise them.
So, Lord being the Supreme Father, the original person,
we being His amsa,
that trait in a pure form is also there in the Lord,
and He gets pleased when a devotee glorifies Him,
praises Him.
And He has unlimited glories, He has unlimited qualities.
Sometimes somebody praises us,
yes, that praise may not be genuine,
or there may be relative praise.
For example, somebody may say,
you are so fine, you are so handsome.
Somebody may say that, but then it's a relative truth.
Today a person may be handsome,
tomorrow after 10 years, 15 years, that beauty is gone.
Somebody is rich today, wealth is gone.
But that's not the case with the Lord.
So, you see all exalted devotees glorifying the Lord,
praising the Lord for all the exalted qualities He has.
So, that's important component of the prayer,
glorification of the Lord.
Another aspect I said, going through proper channel,
going through Guru, then Gauranga, then Krishna,
not directly jump to the Krishna.
Even in the company for that matter,
if there is a whole structure,
although that's not a good example,
but just to get a sense.
For every small thing, you don't go to the chairman.
If you have to go to the chairman,
there is a proper channel.
You go to your manager,
the manager in turn speaks to the thing.
And if at all you want to meet the chairman,
you can go through them.
Then another important aspect of the prayer is
revealing the mind, the heart.
Like Arjuna did in the battlefield of Kurukshetra.
He revealed what he is going through,
his feelings, his emotion, what he is going through.
I am confused.
I am no longer sure of what I should be doing.
My mind is reeling.
I am unable to stand any longer.
My bow, Gandiva, is slipping from my hand.
He reveals his mind, reveals his heart.
Although the Lord is all-knowing,
but nevertheless we can, in our prayers,
reveal that I am feeling weak.
I am feeling helpless.
Although I have everything,
but I am seeing that all this is of no use.
So reveal our mind, reveal our heart,
reveal our...
Even if we are feeling weak,
there is no harm in revealing.
Sometimes it says,
no, no, no, we shouldn't reveal our weakness.
We always project something what we are not.
Otherwise people will take undue advantage of my weakness.
There was a person who came to meet me.
He was actually in a very, very bad financial state.
He didn't even have
money to pay the monthly rentals
for the house in which he was staying.
But for the last eight months or one year,
he was projecting,
because his daughter has to get married,
he was projecting as if everything is okay.
Although he is borrowing money from some people,
but it's all very secretly done.
Although he wants to sell the car,
but he is not selling the car.
Otherwise people will get a sense that
he is in bad financial state.
So many times we maintain a farce
to cover up the reality.
So when we pray to the Lord,
when we approach the Lord,
we don't have to have some kind of screens
covering the reality.
We can reveal our heart,
reveal our mind,
reveal the reality.
Our true feelings with the Lord.
Sometimes people say,
power of positive thinking,
we should think positive.
If you are feeling weak,
we can say that,
yes, I am feeling weak.
I am feeling nervous.
So reveal the mind.
Another important component of the prayer is
gratitude.
One is expressing the glorification of the Lord.
Another is,
for all what the Lord has done for us,
He is continuing to do for us,
the strength He has bestowed us,
all the resources what we have,
we have to recognize them
and we have to express gratitude,
thanks.
Otherwise,
suppose a person has given you something,
an employer has given you something and
you are always cribbing and
I don't have this,
or asking for more,
without recognizing that
I have been blessed with so many things.
So another important component is,
another important component of the prayer is
glorification,
gratitude.
It's also called thanksgiving.
Again, it's a very quote unquote
Christian terminology.
To express our thanks,
gratitude.
So that's another important component of the prayer.
And last but not the least,
we have some specific requests.
Just like Arjuna,
please guide me.
Now I am a soul surrendered unto Him.
Please instruct me.
Please tell me for sure what I should do.
I am in dilemma.
I am in confusion.
So he is requesting Lord for guidance,
for specific clear instructions
as to how we should proceed from that point.
So we will have kirtans now
and after the kirtans,
we will,
I will share with you,
I will share with you some very authorized prayers
from Bhagavad Gita,
from Bhagavatam,
from our authorized scriptures,
which will give us some sense of the components
which we have so far discussed.
The components of faith,
components of glorification,
the components of gratitude,
the component of revealing our mind,
component of making a request.
But in all the prayers you will see
that request is in the last.
In other words,
it's just last 5 or 10% of the prayer.
Otherwise,
if you don't know how to pray,
mostly our prayer starts with a request.
No glorification,
no gratitude,
no expressing our thanks,
no revealing our mind,
not going through proper channel,
directly we say,
Oh Lord,
give me this,
as if He is our order carrier.
Again,
this is not somehow to make our request get accepted.
In fact,
a devotee knows,
even if he is making some specific request or prayers,
he always adds,
Oh Lord,
please,
I am needing this,
I want this,
if you so desire.
He adds this phrase,
knowing that,
who knows,
Lord has better plans for me,
I have limited understanding,
as to what is good for me,
what I need.
For example,
somebody is praying,
I want this specific job.
Who knows,
the Lord knows that,
yes,
if a person gets this job,
he'll get more and more entangled,
and whatever relationship he has,
will get completely finished.
He may not want to sanction that job,
but if we are persistent,
if we are just insisting,
by hook or crook,
I want this job,
then Lord may sanction.
He is all powerful,
but then it may not be in our self-interest.
So devotee,
even if,
because of attachment,
he makes some specific prayers,
but he always ends such prayers with,
if you so desire,
if that is your will,
if that is what pleases you.
So we will continue our discussion,
we will get into some specific prayers,
which devotees have offered in the Bhagavad Gita,
in the Bhagavatam,
and we'll do some bit more analysis,
and we'll have some kirtans now,
and after that,
we will get into further discussion.
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much
Thank you..

if I am born as an insect let me be born in the house of your devotees let me be
in association of your devotees is all wonderful prayers with wonderful
feelings these all prayers some elements have taken from the prayers
offered by great exalted personalities exalted Vaishnavas Oh Lord of Madhu Lord
Krishna's killed the Madhu demon just like the Ganges perpetually flow
towards the sea you know when you have seen a river with currents moving gushing
with lot of force just like the Ganges perpetually flow towards the sea you know when you have seen a river with currents moving gushing with lot of force just like
the Ganges perpetually move towards the sea likewise let my attraction be
constantly drawn towards you without getting distracted by anyone else these
are all elements from exalted prayers offered by great devotees so I will
share with you a few authorized prayers I have compiled some in fact I am going
to share with you a PDF which you can download
and some of these prayers you can also start offering regularly before I share
